name: Lamb with Orzo Paste, Greek Style
recipeInstructions:
1. In a stove-top Dutch oven, brown the boneless lamb shoulder in olive oil. Salt and pepper to taste.
2. Add enough stock so that it comes halfway up the side of the meat, then cover and simmer until very tender (about two hours).
3. Remove the meat from the pot and set aside, covered, to keep warm. Strain the fat from the stock and add more if necessary to make four cups. Set the strained stock aside.
4. Heat the Dutch oven again and add half a cup olive oil. Add the orzo paste and toss for a minute.
5. Add the chopped onion and sauté until the onion is clear. Stir in the tomatoes, dillweed, and parsley.
6. Return the meat to the pot along with the four cups of stock. Cover and simmer until the broth is absorbed and the pasta is tender (about 30 minutes). If the dish is too wet when the pasta is tender, continue cooking for a few more minutes with the lid off to reduce the sauce.
~ Jeff Smith "The Frugal Gourmet"
